content:
First, place the rectangle so that  
A=(0,0), B=(5,0), C=(5,12), D=(0,12).  
Since E has distance 12 below AB (the x–axis) and distance 1 from AD (the y–axis), and ∆ABE is acute, one finds  
E=(1,−12).  

A line through E that meets AB at F=(t,0) and CD at G=(s,12) must have the same slope FE as FG.  Hence  
s=2t−1.  

That line cuts the rectangle into two regions.  The “top‐right” region F–B–C–G is a quadrilateral whose area (by shoelace) is  
½·|120−12(s+t)|,  
and we set this =30 (half of 60).  Since s+t=3t−1 this gives  
120−12(3t−1)=60 ⇒36t=72 ⇒t=2 ⇒s=3.  

Hence F=(2,0), G=(3,12) and  
FG²=(3−2)²+(12−0)²=1+144=145.  

Therefore the answer is  
\boxed{145}.

A=(0,0), B=(5,0), C=(5,12), D=(0,12).  
Since E has distance 12 below AB (the x–axis) and distance 1 from AD (the y–axis), and ∆ABE is acute, one finds  
E=(1,−12).  

A line through E that meets AB at F=(t,0) and CD at G=(s,12) must have the same slope FE as FG.  Hence

That line cuts the rectangle into two regions.  The “top‐right” region F–B–C–G is a quadrilateral whose area (by shoelace) is

and we set this =30 (half of 60).  Since s+t=3t−1 this gives

FG²=(3−2)²+(12−0)²=1+144=145.  

Therefore the answer is  
\boxed{145}.
